text:
I'm sorry to hear about the exam. Please know that one failed test, even an important one, does not define your ability, your worth, or the trajectory of where you're headed. It measures one performance on one day, under one particular set of circumstances, and that is a very narrow slice of who you actually are.

It's completely okay to feel disappointed right now. You worked toward something and hoped for a different result, and the sting of that is real. Give yourself a little room to feel it without piling on the harsh self-judgment. Falling short of a goal is not the same as being a failure, even though the two can feel identical in the first raw hours.

When you're ready, this setback can become useful information rather than just a wound. You'll be able to look at what happened with clearer eyes, figure out what tripped you up, and approach the next attempt with a sharper sense of what you need. Some of the most capable people you'll ever meet have a string of failures behind them that they simply learned to treat as steps rather than endings. A second attempt with hard-won insight is often stronger than a first attempt fueled by hope alone.

For now, be as kind to yourself as you would be to a friend in your shoes. Rest, take a breath, and remember that this moment is temporary. You are still entirely capable of getting where you want to go, and one exam has not taken that away from you. I'm rooting for you.
